dashboardAnnotation: From manual monthly review decks to one portfolio-wide control tower.

chatScripts.userQuery: Why did NPL spike in the SME segment last month?
chatScripts.aiResponse: SME NPL rose from 2.1% to 3.4% month-over-month. Three drivers: concentration in Surabaya branch (41% of new delinquencies), a cohort of loans originated in Q4 2025 with DSR above 55% (~230 accounts), and delayed collections SLA averaging 9 days vs. 4-day target.
followUps: What can we do about the Q4 cohort?

chatScripts.userQuery: What can we do about the Q4 cohort?
chatScripts.aiResponse: Start with the ~230 Q4 2025 SME accounts where DSR exceeds 55%; 78% of current delinquencies sit in this group. Reprice or restructure the top 120 high-exposure accounts first, then apply tighter DSR caps for renewals. This can lower projected new NPL additions by about 40% over the next two quarters.

New SME delinquencies total IDR 43.9B last month: Surabaya IDR 18.0B (41%), Jakarta IDR 9.2B (21%), Medan IDR 6.1B (14%), Bandung IDR 4.8B (11%), and other branches IDR 5.8B (13%). Surabaya also has the slowest collections SLA at 9 days, versus the 4-day target.

recommendations.headline: Tighten DSR cap to 50% for SME loans above IDR 500M
recommendations.impact: Est. 40% reduction in new NPL additions
recommendations.rationale: Based on: Q4 2025 cohort analysis — 78% of delinquencies had DSR > 55%

recommendations.headline: Deploy collections task force to Surabaya branch
recommendations.rationale: Based on: 41% delinquency concentration and 9-day average SLA breach

recommendations.headline: Introduce real-time DSR monitoring for SME portfolio
recommendations.rationale: Based on: pattern of DSR-to-NPL conversion in Q4 cohort
